title: Jaylen Brown trade grades: Celtics get poor marks from multiple outlets for ‘baffling’ deal
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks.
Media outlets gave the Celtics poor grades for the trade, criticizing the exchange for Brown's MVP-caliber performance.
Critics highlighted the risk Boston takes with an aging Paul George and potential short-term impact on Jayson Tatum's prime.

title: What led to the Celtics trading Jaylen Brown? Recapping a drama-filled offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks.
Brown's offseason was marked by critical comments about officiating and a public feud with media pundits.
Despite expressing a desire to stay, tensions with the Celtics contributed to Brown's departure.

title: 6 takeaways from the U.S. World Cup win over Bosnia-Herzegovina
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The U.S. men's soccer team defeated Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 in a World Cup Round of 16 match in San Francisco.
Goals by Folarin Balogun and Malik Tillman secured the U.S.'s first knockout stage win since 2002.
The U.S. will face Belgium on July 6 in Seattle after advancing past Bosnia-Herzegovina.

title: Brighton man charged in 2025 killing of 15-year-old in Allston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Armani Collazos, 26, of Brighton, charged with murder in 2025 death of Phillip Franco, 15, in Allston.
Collazos faces various charges including murder, firearm offenses, and assault with intent to murder.
Miranda Bodden, 22, of Allston, charged as accessory after the fact, released on $2,000 bail.

title: Bus driver strike disrupts bus service in Merrimack Valley
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit workers, including bus drivers, began a strike Wednesday affecting bus services in several Massachusetts towns.
The strike is a response to proposed management changes reducing overtime pay and increasing weekend shifts for less senior employees.
MeVa management offered a 25% pay raise over three years, while negotiations with Teamsters Local 170 continue to resolve the strike.

title: Charania: Jaylen Brown ‘never requested a trade’ from Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown reportedly never requested a trade from the Boston Celtics, according to ESPN NBA insider Shams Charania.
The Celtics traded Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers, despite his desire to remain with the team.
Boston's decision to trade followed unsuccessful attempts to acquire Giannis Antetokounmpo using Brown as trade bait.

title: 2 men involved in 2015 Dorchester home invasion, murder sentenced after conviction, guilty plea
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Victor Arrington was sentenced to life in prison without parole for a 2015 Dorchester home invasion and murder.
James Boyd received a sentence of time served and five years probation after pleading guilty to several charges.
The 2015 crime involved a mistaken home invasion resulting in the murder of Richard Long and injury to his fiancée.

title: What to know about heat warnings, cooling centers in Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu issued an excessive heat warning through Friday with temperatures expected to reach a heat index of 112 degrees.
Cooling centers, pools, libraries, and cultural institutions in Boston offer relief, with adjusted hours and free admissions during the heat wave.
Emergency shelters are open 24 hours for homeless individuals, with a temporary amnesty period implemented due to high temperatures.

title: Dartmouth Mall rolls out new limits on youth visiting mall without an adult
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dartmouth Mall will require those under 18 to be with an adult after 5 p.m. on weekends.
The policy, effective July 3, applies to all areas of the mall except stores with external entrances.
Reactions to the new policy on Facebook are mixed, with some applauding safety measures and others expressing nostalgia.

title: Bruins reportedly acquire defenseman Will Borgen in trade with Rangers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded for defenseman Will Borgen from the Rangers, giving up a 2027 second-round and a 2028 conditional third-round pick.
Will Borgen, 29, known for his physical play and mobility, is expected to fill a top-four defensive role for the Bruins.
Borgen appeared in 75 games for the Rangers last season, scoring five goals and 10 assists, and is signed for four years with a $4.1 million cap hit.
